description: A cropped screenshot of a New York Post web article styled as a shareable graphic. At the top is the recognizable red New York Post masthead bar with a hamburger menu icon, an envelope icon, the white slanted 'NEW YORK POST' logo, and a 'LOG IN' button at right; below it a white navigation row with section tabs 'US News', 'Metro' (active, red-underlined), 'Long Island', 'Politics', 'World Ne[ws]'. The body shows a red 'METRO' tag and red italic 'EXCLUSIVE' label, followed by the large bold black headline 'Inside ICE operations in New Jersey — as agents nab two child predators.' The byline 'By Reuven Fenton and Alex Oliveira' (names in red) and 'Published July 21, 2025, 7:22 p.m. ET' appear beneath, with a small red comment-count badge '61' in the lower right. This is a clean media-clip card rather than an original poster; ICE is amplifying favorable third-party press coverage. The messaging technique is borrowed-credibility / earned-media amplification, leaning on the 'child predators' framing to associate immigration enforcement with protecting children. The accompanying tweet adds a scoreboard-style taunt ('Illegal alien child predators in NJ: 0 / @ICEgov: 2'). Target audience: general public and supporters, signaling press validation of ICE's mission.
notable_text: NEW YORK POST | METRO EXCLUSIVE | Inside ICE operations in New Jersey — as agents nab two child predators
